text.title: Agentic Commerce Timeline and Metrics
text.summary: Projected 2026-2028 adoption timeline for agentic commerce and the five metrics to track readiness: AI referral traffic, SGE appearance rate, review score average, entity recognition accuracy, and API usage.

list.item: 25% of organic traffic shifts to AI assistants.
Google SGE becomes default search interface.
Agentic commerce early adoption in B2B SaaS and local services.
LLM-mediated traffic overtakes traditional organic search.
Traditional SEO effectiveness declines for discovery (retains value for destination traffic).
Autonomous purchasing normalized for repeat and commodity purchases.
Direct AI-to-API commerce for structured product/service data.
Brand authority becomes primary ranking signal over technical SEO.
Human verification required only for high-stakes transactions.

rule.statement: Track five agentic commerce readiness metrics monthly: AI referral traffic (ChatGPT/Perplexity/Claude referrals), SGE appearance rate (target >50%), review score average across platforms (target >4.5/5), entity recognition accuracy in knowledge panels (target 100%), API usage if applicable.
rule.rationale: Traditional SEO metrics (rankings, organic traffic) do not capture AI-mediated discovery. These five metrics directly reflect the factors AI agents use for provider selection. Monthly tracking enables early detection of visibility loss as AI search share increases.

antipattern.description: Continuing to optimize only for keyword rankings while AI search share grows.
antipattern.reason: Keyword rankings measure one channel that is shrinking. Businesses that ignore AI search metrics will see total discovery traffic decline even if keyword rankings hold.

antipattern.description: Assuming agentic commerce only affects B2C e-commerce.
antipattern.reason: B2B SaaS, professional services, and local services are early agentic commerce adoption areas. AI agents researching CRM solutions or booking tradespeople are already active in 2026.
